summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Iteration 8: Granular Permissions System. All 8 backend API tests passed. All frontend UI tests passed. The permissions system correctly restricts access based on user permissions.

issues: When a user without dashboard:ver permission logs in, the Dashboard view still loads initially (though sidebar correctly hides Dashboard link). Should redirect to first permitted module.

passed_tests: GET /api/permisos/modulos returns 12 modules with correct actions
Creating user with role 'usuario' shows permissions grid with 12 modules
Permissions grid has correct actions per module (some only 'ver', others have full CRUD)
Toggling 'Todo' checkbox enables/disables all actions for that module
Disabling 'Ver' disables all other actions for that module
Enabling any action auto-enables 'Ver' for that module
Saving permissions via PUT /api/usuarios/{id} persists correctly
Created testperm user with limited permissions (productos:ver, clientes:ver,crear)
Login as testperm - sidebar only shows Productos and Clientes (permitted modules)
Login as testperm - Productos view does NOT show create/edit/delete buttons
Login as testperm - Clientes view shows 'Nuevo Cliente' button but NOT delete buttons
Admin (andy.escudero) sees ALL 12 modules in sidebar
Admin sees all action buttons (create/edit/delete) in all views
Usuarios view shows 'Permisos' column with count for non-admin users
Admin users show 'Acceso total' instead of permissions count
Clicking permisos button opens modal with permissions editor for existing users
Permission-based API access control works (403 for unauthorized actions)
action_items: MINOR: Consider redirecting users without dashboard:ver permission to their first permitted module on login
critical_code_review_comments: The hasPermission helper function correctly returns true for admins always
The sidebar check function correctly uses permisos[modulo].ver to determine visibility
MODULOS_CONFIG in frontend matches MODULOS_PERMISOS in backend (12 modules)
The PermisosEditor component correctly handles Ver/Todo toggle logic
seed_data_creation: Created testperm user with limited permissions for UI testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 8 granular permissions system fully tested. Key features: (1) GET /api/permisos/modulos returns 12 modules with their available actions. (2) Creating/editing users with role='usuario' shows permissions grid. (3) Permissions are persisted via PUT /api/usuarios/{id}. (4) Sidebar hides modules user can't see (checks permisos[modulo].ver). (5) Action buttons (create/edit/delete) are hidden based on permissions. (6) Admins have full access regardless of permisos field. Test user 'testperm' (password: 1234) exists with limited permissions for testing.
